name: Ashkenazic Chicken Soup & Matzo Balls with Fresh Dill
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken wings or drumsticks in a large pot and cover with 9 cups cold water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to maintain a simmer.
2. Add onion, carrot, parsnip (if using), celery stalks with tops, parsley sprigs, and dill sprigs to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Simmer for about 1 hour or until chicken is cooked through.
4. Strain broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
5. For matzo balls, mix together matzo meal, egg, parsley, dill, salt, and water in a bowl until just combined.
6. Form the mixture into 1-inch balls and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up.
7. Bring broth back to a simmer in the pot. Gently drop matzo balls into the boiling soup using a slotted spoon.
8. Simmer for another 15-20 minutes, or until matzo balls float to the surface.
9. Remove matzo balls from the soup and serve hot with snipped fresh dill on top.
